claimText: Arogyapacha is an indigenous medicinal plant of India traditionally used by the Kani tribes.
evidenceExcerpt: A rare, indigenous medicinal plant of India... Famous for traditional use by Kani tribes
examUse: Match the following (Tribe vs. Plant) or statement-based question on traditional knowledge.

claimText: The Kani tribes, known for their traditional use of Arogyapacha, are located in the Agastya hills of the Western Ghats.
evidenceExcerpt: Famous for traditional use by Kani tribes, in Agastya hills, Western Ghats.

claimText: The drug 'Jeevani' has been developed using the medicinal plant Arogyapacha.
evidenceExcerpt: 'Jeevani' Drug has been developed using 'Arogya Pacha'.
examUse: Statement-based question on the applications of indigenous flora.

claimText: The Kani tribes receive a 50 percent share of the commercial returns from the commercialization of Arogyapacha.
evidenceExcerpt: Kani Tribes gets a share of 50% on commercial returns.
examUse: Statement-based question on Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) under biodiversity frameworks.
